zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John St. John於1562年10月14日出生在英國維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲時，他的父親Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire35歲，母親Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze22歲。他於1582年10月14日與Countess Lucy Hungerford在英國英格蘭維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1594年9月20日在他的家鄉享年31歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John St. John于1562年10月14日出生在英国維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲时，他的父亲Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire35岁，母亲Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze22岁。他于1582年10月14日与Countess Lucy Hungerford在英国英格兰維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1594年9月20日在他的家乡享年31岁，埋葬在英国維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲。

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  French: habitational name from any of the places so called, for example in Normandy, named from a religious dedication to a (male) St. Clair.
2  North American variant spelling of   St  .   Claire  .

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: St
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Ancar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Alphonse, Etienne, Fabien, Mireille.

Probably an altered form of French (Pas-de-Calais, Nord) Ancart, itself a variant of Hancart and Hanecart: from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Han, a short form of French Jehan and Flemish Johan(nes) (see John ).

variants.name: Bongiovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Antonio, Dante, Salvatore, Santo, Antonino, Domenic, Gaetano, Geno, Giacomo, Gilda, Luciano, Nino.
Italian: from a personal name composed of the elements bon ‘good’ + Giovanni, an equivalent of John .

variants.name: Di Giovanna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Alfonso, Carlo, Domenic, Gaetano, Liborio, Nunzio, Pietro, Rosario, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: metronymic from the female personal name Giovanna, an equivalent of Joan (see John ).

variants.name: Anes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Angel, Dionisio, Esperanza, Gilberto, Ines, Juanita, Luis, Luisa, Manuel, Manuela, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from a shortened form of the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Burhans
meanings: German: from Middle Low German būr ‘farmer, peasant’ + the personal name Hans, a short form of Johannes (see John ), hence a distinguishing occupational name ‘farmer John’ with Hans being a very frequent name. See also Burian .

variants.name: Breihan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt.
German: distinguishing nickname for a (beer) brewer, from Middle High German briuw(er) ‘brew(er)’ + a short form of the personal name Johann(es) (see John ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Middle English male personal name Jan(e), from Old French Jehan, a variant of Johan, which gave rise to the common alternative John . Sometimes the name may also arise from the Welsh variant Ieun of Welsh Ieuan, likewise equivalent to John (compare Evan ).

variants.name: Di Giovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Vito, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carlo, Gino, Giuseppe, Guido, Agostino, Carmela.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Giovanni, an equivalent of John . Compare De Giovanni , Dejohn , and Giovanni .

variants.name: Eanes
meanings: English (Hampshire): probably a variant of Heanes with loss of initial H-, itself a variant of Heane, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a nickname from Middle English hen(e), heine ‘poor, needy, wretched’ (Old English hēan). Eanes is now rare in Britain, and in the US is found predominantly in VA.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Ene
meanings: Romanian: from a derivative of the personal name Ioan (see John ).
West African (Nigeria): unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John St. John was born on 14 October 1562, in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, his father, Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire, was 35 and his mother, Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze, was 22. He married Countess Lucy Hungerford on 14 October 1582, in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 20 September 1594, in his hometown, at the age of 31, and was buried in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John St. John est né le 14 octobre 1562, à Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Countess Lucy Hungerford le 14 octobre 1582, à Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 20 septembre 1594, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 31 ans, et a été enterré à Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John St. John am 14. Oktober 1562 in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire 35 und seine Mutter Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze 22. Er hat am 14. Oktober 1582 in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom Countess Lucy Hungerford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 20. September 1594 im Alter von 31 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John St. John è nato il il 14 ottobre 1562 nel Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Countess Lucy Hungerford il 14 ottobre 1582, nel Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 20 settembre 1594, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 31 anni, e fu sepolto a Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John St. Johnが1562年10月14日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアリディアルド トレゴズで生まれた時，父Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshireは35歳，母Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogozeは22歳であった。1582年10月14日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアリディアルド トレゴズでCountess Lucy Hungerfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1594年9月20日に故郷で31歳で死去し，イギリスウィルトシュアイアリディアルド トレゴズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John St. John(이)가 1562년 10월 14일 영국 윌츠히르 리디아르드 트레고즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1582년 10월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 리디아르드 트레고즈에서 Countess Lucy Hungerford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1594년 9월 20일 그의 고향에서 31세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르 리디아르드 트레고즈에 묻혔다.

pt.14 October 1562–20 September 1594: 14 de outubro de 1562–20 de setembro de 1594

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John St. John nasceu em 14 de outubro de 1562, em Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Countess Lucy Hungerford em 14 de outubro de 1582, em Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 20 de setembro de 1594, em sua cidade natal, com 31 anos, e foi sepultado em Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John St. John родился 14 октября 1562 г. в местности лидярд трегоз, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Countess Lucy Hungerford 14 октября 1582 г. в местности лидярд трегоз, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 20 сентября 1594 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 31 год и был похоронен в местности лидярд трегоз, уилцхир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John St. John nació el 14 de octubre de 1562, en Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Countess Lucy Hungerford el 14 de octubre de 1582, en Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 20 de septiembre de 1594, en su pueblo, a la edad de 31 años, y fue sepultado en Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.
